false_positives.what_happened: The majority of the 59 hits are within-file self-similarity inside vendored amalgamated single-header libraries: nlohmann/json v3.11.2, Pantor inja v3.4.0, quickjspp. These headers contain many near-identical template specializations (serializer/parser overloads) by design, so a within-file token detector finds dozens of high-similarity blocks that are not authored duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: vendoring filter by path (include/nlohmann/**, include/inja.hpp, include/quickjspp.hpp) and/or detect amalgamated single-header libs via SPDX/copyright banner in the first ~30 lines; exclude their within-file self-matches from the copypaste count.

false_positives.where: src/handler/webget.cpp:210-231 <- 232-247 and similar author-code within-file pairs
false_positives.what_happened: The few author-code hits (subexport.cpp, templates.cpp, webget.cpp) are short adjacent blocks with low line-similarity (0.5-0.6) -- repeated curl/option-setup and template-render boilerplate, not copied-and-edited logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: raise the min line-similarity floor for within-file pairs in the same function neighborhood, or require a minimum normalized-token span before counting a within-file hit.

note: 59 hits = vendored single-header self-similarity (nlohmann/inja/quickjspp) + a little author boilerplate. No authored copypaste. sccs=0.
